titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Dewey-Lowell Family Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Kolbe Resnick

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0
This collection contains 1 published digital object. And that object are comprised of 4 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday October 8, 2025 at 23:18

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Dewey-Lowell Family Papers consists of correspondence, manuscripts, ephemera, photographs, notebooks, scrapbooks, and other documents created by, used, or possessed by members of the Dewey and Lowell families in New England during the 19th century. Many of the materials relate to, or are directly linked or created by notable politicians, abolitionists, writers, preachers, and businessmen from the mid to late 19th century. The collection spans the years 1779-1942, with the bulk of the material relating to the Civil War and life during the surrounding years.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Cumberland Rare Books on the Henry C. Taylor Fund, Danford N. Barney, Jr. Fund, Alfred Z. Baker Fund, Elinor and Leonard Bernheim Fund, Stanton Francis Kennedy Fund, and the Jack Callaway Fund, 2023.
October 2023 acquisition purchased from Swann galleries.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The Dewey-Lowell family papers are arranged into six series based on material type:

Series I. Notebooks, scrapbooks and other bound material, circa 1796-1933 Series II. Correspondence, circa 1777-1942 Series III. Manuscripts, circa 1548-1901 Series IV. Ephemera, circa 1748-1891 Series V. Deeds and related land acquisition documents, 1779-1816 Series VI. Photographs, circa 1880-1903 Series VII. October 2023 acquisition, 1812-1899

All series are arranged chronologically with the exception of Series III, Manuscripts, which is arranged by author last name. Series II, Correspondence, is divided into two sub-series based on family.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Born in 1794 in Sheffield, Massachusetts, Orville Dewey was a famous Unitarian preacher, who began his career in New Bedford as the Pastor of the Unitarian Church from 1823 to 1833. In 1835, he joined the ministry at the Second Unitarian Church of New York, overseeing the construction of the Church of the Messiah (following the destruction of his church due to a fire), located at 728-30 Broadway. Resigning in 1848, Dewey took time to attend to a chronic health condition, traveling to Europe for treatment as he would numerous times during his life. He picked up again with a series of lectures at the Lowell Institute in 1855. Between 1858 and 1862 he was the pastor of the New South Church in Boston, where he eventually retired to live on his home in Sheffield, dying in 1882. Among Dewey's descendants are his daughter, Mary Elizabeth Dewey (1821-1910), a noted author and translator and Charles Orville Dewey (1831-1901), a soldier with the Massachusetts 49th Infantry during the Civil War.

One of the Boston Brahmin families, the Lowell Family descended from John Lowell of Newburyport (1743-1802). With many famous descendants, the family became well known and intertwined with other literary, political, educational, and religious circles. Among some of the well-known family members are William Lowell Putnam, Mary Lowell Putnam, James Russell Lowell, Augustus Lowell, Charles Lowell, Robert Traill Spence Lowell, John Lowell, Jr., Amy Lowell, and Robert Lowell.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Dewey-Lowell Family Papers consists of correspondence, manuscripts, ephemera, photographs, notebooks, scrapbooks, and other documents related to the ancestors and descendants of Orville Dewey and the Lowell family. Alongside individual entries of daily life, the scrapbooks and notebooks include newspaper clippings, advertisements, quotes, photographs, and ephemera pasted in, as well as pressed plants. The correspondence deals largely with family matters, news, and professional and personal engagements, specifically in the years preceding, during, and following the American Civil War. The notebooks and scrapbooks are largely compiled by Mary L. Dewey and many include materials created by or related to her father, Charles Orville Dewey, and grandfather, Orville Dewey. Of particular interest are the family's relationships with famous literary and political figures of their time, including Daniel Webster, Henry Wadsworth Longfellow, Horace Greeley, Isabella Stewart Gardner, Nathaniel Hawthorne, Oliver Wendell Holmes, Ralph Waldo Emerson, Wendell Phillips, William Cullen Bryant, George Washington, Sarah Chauncey Woolsey, and William Henry Furness. Also included are deeds and land acquisition documents (dating from 1779 to 1816) related to Silas Dewey, Orville Dewey's father, loose photographs and albums documenting different generations of the Dewey family and their lives (as well as early photographic processes) and a manuscript map of The Siege of Algiers, 1816 .

October 2023 acquisition consists of correspondence, Orville Dewey's diary dating from 1865 to 1880, a 26-page manuscript titled Oration Delivered on the 4th of July, 1811 on the 35th Year of the American Independence, clippings, an ink drawing of Dewey's home in Sheffield, Massachusetts, and a large scrapbook with documents related to family history. This addition also includes material documenting and related to marriage ceremonies performed by Orville Dewey between the years 1826 and 1833.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Dewey-Lowell Family Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Dewey-Lowell Family Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

did.unittitle: Notebooks, scrapbooks, and other bound material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I contains writings from members of both families documented in this collection. Primarily represented are writings from the Dewey family. In addition to Mary Dewey's own notebooks and journal, are scrapbooks of writings, clippings, ephemera, and other material she compiled, consisting of pasted in letter fragments, journal entries, drafts, and sermons by Orville Dewey and Charles Orville Dewey. The scrapbooks in the series also contain materials from travels in the West Indies, poetry manuscript material, and quotes from historical figures and various epitaphs.

In addition to the writings and correspondence fragments in this series, the scrapbooks include realia, ephemera, and pressed leaves, mostly compiled by Mary L. Dewey and Charles Orville Dewey. Also included are diaries and notebooks, including Charles Orville Dewey's diary from his time in the Civil War (also including pressed leaves) and Mary L. Dewey's notebooks of poems and journal entries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pasted in letters, envelopes, manuscripts, and notes from both Dewey and Lowell families. Includes materials related to Louisa May Alcott, Horace Greeley, and Caroline Kirkland, among others. Includes a autographed envelope sent by George Washington.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 1, 2, 11, 12, 15, 19, and 20: In-process material pending conservation treatment.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings from Orville Dewey's diaries. Contains letter fragments and Mary L. Dewey's last will and testament.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings from Charles Orville Dewey's diaries.

The Works of Orville Dewey, with a Biographical Sketch , inscribed

scopecontent.p: Diary entries compiled with poems and quotations, pasted in clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings from Charles Orville Dewey's diaries, inscribed "from the diary of Charles Orville Dewey, 1899-1900."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Travel diary scrapbook from trip to the West Indies, includes postcards, ephemera, clippings, plant life.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 1, 2, 11, 12, 15, 19, and 20: In-process material pending conservation treatment.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into two sub-series based on family. Each sub-series is arranged in chronological order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II contains letters (and in some cases the corresponding envelope) addressed to and from members of each family. Spanning multiple generations of Deweys and Lowells, the bulk of the letters in this series date between 1840-1900. Many of the letters concern the Civil War and life in New England during those years. Also included is correspondence from noteworthy historical figures of the time period.

Jameson, Anna to Mary Lowell Putnam
Mohl, Mary to Mary Lowell Putnam

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by author last name, with manuscripts by unidentified authors at the end of the series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III consists of manuscripts written by some of the most famous figures of the era, including Ralph Waldo Emerson, Sarah Chauncey Woolsey, and William Henry Furness. Most of the manuscripts are poetry, in the author's hand, but also included are speeches and song lyrics.

did.unittitle: [We all must fight our dragon...], autograph manuscript
[That women sometimes change 'tis true…], autograph manuscript
Poem for Pierre Guynet , autograph manuscript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series IV consists of ephemera from both Dewey and Lowell families. Included are tickets for a ship, Civil War passes, receipts for donations, clippings, and other printed material dating from 1748 to 1891.

did.unittitle: Introductory lecture at Apollo Association, Dewey, Orville
Boarding School for Young Ladies ephemera
New York Genealogical and Biographical Record
Memorial Ode to Thomas Wentworth Higginson
James Russell Lowell Memoriam, Holmes, Olive Wendell
Receipt for donation to Plymouth Monument
Deeds and related land acquisition documents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series V includes letters and related land deeds between the years 1779 to 1816. These pertain largely to the Dewey family, most notably Orville Dewey's father, Silas Dewey.

did.unittitle: Deed, Fowle, Daniel to Keith Spence
Deed, Forbes, Samuel to Silas Dewey, Thomas Stevens, and Sylvester Root
Deed, Gleason, Arial to Thomas Stevens
Deed, Noble, Russell to Silas Dewey
Deed, Callender, Aaron to Silas Dewey
Deed, Callender, Aaron to Silas Dewey

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically, with undated photographs listed at the end of the series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series VI contains photographs of the Dewey family. Many of the photographs are formal portraits of family members, most notably the immediate family members of Charles Orville Dewey and Harriet Dewey, with some more candid family group photographs included within the photograph albums.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tintype, collodion on paper prints

did.unittitle: Dewey, Russell, Dewey, Harriet, and Dewey, William

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collodion on paper prints

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of correspondence, Orville Dewey's diary dating from 1865 to 1880, a 26-page manuscript titled Oration Delivered on the 4th of July, 1811 on the 35th Year of the American Independence, clippings, an ink drawing of Dewey's home in Sheffield, Massachusetts, and a large scrapbook with documents related to family history. This addition also includes material documenting and related to marriage ceremonies performed by Orville Dewey between the years 1826 and 1833.

Chadwick, John White to Mary Dewey
